PLANNER / M4. WEBINAR CONTENT PLANNER
WHAT ’ S THE BEST OUTLINE AND
TIMELINE FOR MY WEBINAR?
There are 5 segments to the first part of your webinar (prior to the pivot and
the offer):
• 	The Pre-Show
• 	The Introduction
• 	Objectives & Commitment
• 	The Reframe or Opportunity (including Your Story)
• 	Instructional Content
In addition to the pre-show, the first part of your webinar should last
approximately 25 to 35 minutes if you’re planning a 60 minute webinar and 36
to 53 minutes if you’re planning a 90 minute webinar.
For a more detailed timeline for this part of your webinar, see the
Webinar Content Timeline

PLANNER / M4. WEBINAR CONTENT PLANNER
WHAT DO I NEED TO COMMUNICATE
DURING THE CONTENT SEGMENT OF MY
WEBINAR?
What follows is the purpose of each portion of the content segment of your
webinar (see further on in this document for the instructional content
portion), and the key things you want to communicate during each portion.
THE PRE-SHOW
The purpose of the pre-show is to get the audience prepared (great
webinars start off this way from the very first moment)
✓ 	T (meaning start time) minus 30 minutes (and at least 15 minutes before
your “starting now” email goes out): Start the webinar. Test mic, video,
slides and chat
✓ 	T minus 20 minutes: Mute the presenter(s), put “Starting Soon” slide on the
screen with the presenters picture (and a countdown timer)
✓ 	T minus 10 minutes: Keep “Starting Soon” slide up. Unmute mic and start
talking with people. Run a welcome exercise (something that starts the
process of getting them in the mindset they need to buy). For example:
✓ 	“What’s your biggest obstacle?”
✓ 	“What did you discover doing the exercise?”
✓ 	“What’s worked for you?”
✓ 	Your responses to their answers should be encouraging: “You’ve
come to the right place, yes we’re absolutely going to cover that in
this webinar…”

-- 4 of 10 --

PLANNER / M4. WEBINAR CONTENT PLANNER
THE INTRODUCTION
This is where you introduce the topic of the webinar and what you will cover.
The purpose of the introduction is to set you up as the expert, and to
establish an emotional connection. This helps to get prospects in the
mindset they need to buy. One option is the Host Introduction (having
someone else be the host), where the host gives their recommendation of
you, your content, your product (if you do this, the focus should be on selling
you rather than introducing the material).
Notes:
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
OBJECTIVES & COMMITMENT
The purpose of objectives and commitment is to create anticipation and set
expectations. Follow the tone you’ve decided to use that fits your audience
and your personality. This is a place you can be determined, and even a little
aggressive.
✓ 	What you want to communicate to set objectives:
“Here’s what we will accomplish in this webinar / what I’m going to give you /
why it’s vital for you to get this information / here’s how this information has
changed peoples lives, like_____ who_________ by implementing (this thing
that I’ll be showing you today).”
✓ 	What you want to communicate to ask for commitment:
“I’m committed to playing all out for the next 90 minutes, holding nothing
back” or, “Giving you three techniques you can implement and start seeing
results from in the next 48 hours” or, “Demonstrating one specific technique
I’ve only shared with my top $25k/year group of clients that has helped them
achieve______.”
✓ 	What you want to communicate to seed the sale:
“I’ll do everything I can to maximize the value you get out of our limited time
together, and I promise to show you how you can learn more.”

THE REFRAME OR OPPORTUNITY (INCLUDING YOUR STORY)
The Reframe
The reframe changes the frame of reference through which your
prospects view things, causing them to think differently about the problem
or potential solutions, and opening them up to new approaches to solve that
problem (shifting them from thinking of the situation as a challenge into
thinking of it as an opportunity). It starts the story arc and sets you up as a
visionary person they want to follow. If done well, this can become the value
of the entire webinar (meaning they don’t need you to give them more
content, they just want to know how to make your reframe happen in their
life). The more totally new the thought, the better. You want to
communicate:
“The old way isn’t working…” Then compare and contrast (the challenge, the
opportunity). Then share your story. Then create hope and anticipation via a
series of questions that all have yes answers: “Can you see how this changes
everything?”, “Can you see how much easier this is than the old way?”, “Are
you ready to learn how to implement this?”
The Opportunity
The opportunity is an alternative to reframe.
✓ 	Describe the current situation, how it’s affecting the world, then offer a
way to fix it and how the world will be different as a result
✓ 	Use this instead of the reframe when the solution is more obvious, when
they’re just missing the pieces to achieve it, when what you’re presenting
is evolutionary rather than revolutionary, or when there’s no obvious “ah-
ha” moment you can use to hook the audience
✓ 	The risk (with the opportunity) is that your presentation becomes entirely
logical, so you need to bring in emotional aspects (how it’s affecting the

“Can you see how this changes everything?”, “Can you see how much easier
this is than the old way?”, “Are you ready to learn how to implement this?

Your Story
This is included in the Reframe or the Opportunity and establishes an
emotional connection. When done well, your prospects say to themselves,
“They’re just like me, and they solved it… There’s hope… I should pay
attention.” It also provides proof that you know what you’re talking about, and
gets your prospects in the mindset that they need to buy.
✓ 	To do this, use the Hero’s Journey (“I struggled, I failed, it almost cost me
everything… Until I discovered what I’m going to show you today.”)
✓ 	Use pictures to illustrate your story
✓ 	Start the story, but don’t finish it until later in the webinar… Leave the loop
open. You want to communicate:
“Here are the three things I learned… or, “First I did this, this was the result,
then I did that.” Then save at least part of your results for your pivot.
✓ 	End the story with a seed for your sale

PLANNER / M4. WEBINAR CONTENT PLANNER
HOW DO I DELIVER MASSIVE VALUE (IN
A WAY THAT NATURALLY LEADS TO THE
SALE)?
You can deliver massive value in your webinar in a way that naturally leads
to the sale by teaching. This instructional content portion of your webinar
is designed to give your prospects enough information to start down the path
towards goal achievement without being able to go all the way to its end, and
gives them the 3 key proofs they need to have before being comfortable to
buy from you. The 3 key proofs are:
✓ 	“Can I learn and implement this stuff? (particularly important if complex)”
✓ 	“Are you the right person to teach me (as opposed to someone else)”
✓ 	“Is the value I will receive worth the price I’m required to pay? (will my
return justify the expense)”
INSTRUCTIONAL CONTENT OPTIONS
There are 5 options for delivering instructional content (the approach you
choose is not as important as how you teach it):
✓ 	Step-by-step content: Take a key process someone needs to do to
achieve a desired transformation and teach exactly, step-by-step, how to
do it
✓ 	In-depth coverage: Go deep into one key thing/step/activity they need to
implement to achieve their objective (this is similar to step-by-step but
often this is more “what to do” versus “how to do it” content)
✓ 	Tips content: Provide several key pieces of the information that help them
to achieve the transformation
✓ 	Objection Handling: The subjects you teach are specifically chosen to
remove objections they have to buying your product
✓ 	System Overview: Outline your system, solution, or methodology
Caution!
✓ 	Don’t leave them feeling either overwhelmed OR satisfied… You must
show that they still need this and that and the other thing
✓ 	What you choose to teach should be one or a few elements of achieving
the transformation, not the complete system to achieve the
transformation

PLANNER / M4. WEBINAR CONTENT PLANNER
✓ 	You need to clearly point out what they still need to achieve the
transformation (this sets up the pivot to the sale). You want to
communicate:
“What I’ve shown you is only a fraction of what you need to succeed.”

PLANNER / M4. WEBINAR CONTENT PLANNER
WHAT ARE SOME WAYS TO BOOST
SALES DURING THE CONTENT PORTION
OF MY WEBINAR?
There are 5 key webinar elements that boost sales:
✓ 	Proof (such as testimonials and case studies)
✓ 	Early injection of product
✓ 	Conversation (asking them questions, inviting participation)
✓ 	Close problem/solution loops
✓ 	Specific to-do assignments
For more on this topic, see the
Webinar Sales Boosters checklist
